title: Mixed berry pie
subtitle: Double-crust pie with blueberries, strawberries, and raspberries
excerpt: A double-crust pie with three summer berries thickened with cornflour and scented with lemon. The filling stays juicy without being runny. A lattice top is optional but shows the deep purple filling.
sourceNotes: Mixed berry pie is a modern American composition rather than a single regional tradition: older American pies typically use a single fruit. The three-berry combination (blueberry, strawberry, raspberry) became common in American bakeries in the 1990s. This recipe synthesises the double-crust pie method from Joy of Cooking (Rombauer, Becker and Becker, 2006) with the three-berry filling ratio developed in Rose Levy Beranbaum's The Pie and Pastry Bible (1998).

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked, assembled pie for up to 3 months. Bake from frozen, adding 20 minutes to the total bake time.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Pastry can be made 3 days ahead. Assemble and bake on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; start at 200°C for 20 minutes, reduce to 180°C for remaining time
baking.decoratingTechnique: lattice top or solid crust with vents, egg washed and scattered with demerara sugar

content.text: Strawberries have more water than blueberries and raspberries. Halve them rather than leaving them whole: smaller pieces release their liquid faster into the filling during baking, where the cornflour can catch it. Large whole strawberries steam in a pocket of liquid and can make the adjacent pastry soggy.

Wait for the filling to cool and set before cutting. A hot pie will have a runny filling; a completely cooled pie (at least 1 hour) will cut cleanly. For the neatest slices, refrigerate overnight and bring to room temperature for 30 minutes before serving.

attrs.prepNote: beaten with 1 tbsp milk, for egg wash

content.text: Make the pastry and chill, 1 hour

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add 5 to 7 tablespoons of ice-cold water, a tablespoon at a time, until the dough just comes together. Divide into two discs (one slightly larger). Wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.

Toss the {{blueberries}}, halved {{strawberries}}, and {{raspberries}} with the {{caster-sugar}}, {{cornflour}}, {{lemon-juice}}, and {{lemon}} zest. Leave to macerate for 10 minutes. Roll the larger pastry disc to a 30 cm circle and line the pie dish. Fill with the berry mixture. Roll the second disc and lay it over the top, or cut into strips for a lattice. Trim to 2 cm overhang and crimp the edges. Cut vents if using a solid top.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Brush the top crust with {{eggs}} wash and scatter {{demerara-sugar}} over. Bake for 20 minutes at 200°C, then reduce to 180°C for a further 30 to 35 minutes until the crust is deep golden and the filling is bubbling visibly through the vents or lattice. Cool on a rack for at least 1 hour before cutting.
